zh.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II是Lady Mabel Mansell的兒子，於1318年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾諾費爾德。他與Amy結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1366年在英國英格蘭賀福爾享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II是Lady Mabel Mansell的儿子，于1318年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾諾費爾德。他与Amy结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1366年在英国英格兰賀福爾享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Vache
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Amedee, Euclide, Josee, Laurent, Lucien, Yvon.  German Gerhard, Friedrich Wilhelm, Hans Diedrich.  Spanish Altagracia, Jorge, Mario, Pedro.

French (mainly northern; Haché): probably a variant of Aché (influenced by the name in 2 below), which is from the ancient Germanic personal name Achari composed of the elements ac ‘blade’ and hari ‘army’. In North America, however, the name Haché may have been an altered form of French Larché (see Larche 2). Compare Achey , Ashey , and Hachey .
In some cases possibly also French (mainly northern) and Walloon: from Old French hache ‘axe, battleaxe’, hence a metonymic occupational name for someone who made or used axes or battleaxes.

variants.name: Hachey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Cecile, Celina, Emile, Fernande, Napoleon, Placide, Romain.
Altered form of French or French Canadian Haché (see Hache 1). Compare Achey .
History: Bearers of the surname Hachey trace their origin to Michel Haché dit Gallant from QC (see Hache ).

variants.name: Ashey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Placide.
Americanized form of French or French Canadian Haché (see Hache 1) or of its altered form Hachey . Compare Achey .
History: Bearers of the surname Ashey mostly trace their origin to Michel Haché dit Gallant from QC (see Hache ).

variants.name: Achey
meanings: Americanized form of French or French Canadian Haché (see Hache 1) or of its altered form Hachey . Compare Achee and Ashey .
History: Bearers of the surname Achey trace their origin to Michel Haché dit Gallant from QC (see Hache ).

variants.name: Larche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Herve, Lucien.

Shortened form of French Larchevêque: from archevêque ‘archbishop’, with fused definite article l', either denoting a member of an archbishop's household or used as an ironic nickname. This surname is also found among African Americans. Compare Larsh 1.
In some cases probably also French (Larché): regional variant of Larcher ‘the bowman’. Compare Hache 1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II was born in 1318, in Northfield, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Lady Mabel Mansell. He married Amy. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1366, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II est né en 1318, à Northfield, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Lady Mabel Mansell. Il a épousé Amy. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1366, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II wurde 1318 in Northfield, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Lady Mabel Mansell geboren. Er hat Amy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1366 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II è nato il nel 1318 nel Northfield, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Lady Mabel Mansell. Lui sposato Amy. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1366, nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache IIは1318年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースフィールドでLady Mabel Mansellの息子として生まれた。Amyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1366年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II(은)는 1318년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 노르스필드에서 Lady Mabel Mansell의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Amy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1366년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II nasceu em 1318, em Northfield, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Lady Mabel Mansell. Ele casou-se com Amy. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1366, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II родился в 1318 г. в местности нортфиелд, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Его мать звали Lady Mabel Mansell. Он женился на женщине по имени Amy. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1366 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Richard De La Vache II nació en 1318, en Northfield, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Lady Mabel Mansell. Se casó con Amy. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1366, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años.
